questions.question_text: How many total pre-license education hours must a broker candidate complete in Illinois?
questions.explanation: Illinois requires broker candidates to complete 75 total pre-license hours. This includes a 60-hour Pre-License Topics course and a 15-hour Applied Real Estate Principles course, both from IDFPR-approved providers.

questions.question_text: A broker candidate plans to complete all 75 pre-license hours through self-paced online courses. Does this meet Illinois requirements?
questions.correct_answer: No, 15 hours must be classroom or interactive
distractors: Yes, all hours may be completed online
No, 30 hours must be completed in a classroom
Yes, if the provider is IDFPR-approved
questions.explanation: Illinois mandates that at least 15 of the 75 pre-license hours be completed in a classroom or interactive setting. Fully self-paced online delivery does not satisfy this interactive component requirement.

questions.question_text: What additional pre-license education must a managing broker candidate complete beyond the standard broker requirements?
questions.explanation: A managing broker candidate must complete 90 additional hours beyond broker pre-license education. This includes 45 hours of post-license education and 45 hours of managing broker pre-license courses.

questions.question_text: A leasing agent applicant in Illinois must complete how many hours of pre-license education?
questions.explanation: Illinois requires leasing agent applicants to complete only 15 hours of pre-license education. This is significantly less than the 75 hours required for a broker license, reflecting the narrower scope of leasing agent activities.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed pre-license education in January 2024 but has not yet applied for a license. By what date must the candidate apply before the education expires?
questions.explanation: Illinois pre-license education is valid for two years after completion. A candidate who finished coursework in January 2024 must apply for licensure by January 2026 or repeat the education requirements.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education must a newly licensed broker complete during the first renewal period?
questions.explanation: New brokers in Illinois must complete 45 hours of post-license education within their first renewal period. This is structured as three 15-hour courses covering Applied Brokerage Principles, Risk Management and Discipline, and Transactional Issues.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed broker closes several transactions during her first two years but does not complete any post-license education before the renewal deadline. What happens to her license?
distractors: She receives a one-year extension to finish
She may renew by paying a double fee instead
questions.explanation: New brokers must complete 45 hours of post-license education during their first renewal period. Failure to do so results in the license becoming inactive. The broker cannot practice until the education is completed and the license is restored.

questions.question_text: How many continuing education hours must an Illinois broker complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: After completing initial post-license requirements, Illinois brokers must finish 12 hours of continuing education every two-year renewal cycle. This includes mandatory core hours and elective hours from approved providers.

questions.question_text: An Illinois broker completed only elective CE courses but skipped the core requirement including Fair Housing hours. What is the consequence?
distractors: The broker receives a 90-day grace period
The broker pays a late fee only
questions.explanation: Failure to complete all required continuing education, including core hours with Fair Housing content, results in the license becoming inactive. The broker cannot practice until the deficiency is corrected and the license is restored.

questions.question_text: Under current Illinois CE requirements, how many of the core hours must cover Fair Housing topics?
questions.explanation: Illinois requires 6 core hours within the 12-hour CE cycle, and 2 of those core hours must specifically address Fair Housing topics. The remaining core and elective hours cover other mandated and optional subjects.

questions.question_text: Illinois real estate licenses are renewed on what schedule?
questions.explanation: Illinois real estate licenses operate on a two-year renewal term. Licensees must complete all CE requirements and submit renewal applications before the deadline to maintain active status.

questions.question_text: An Illinois broker lets her renewal deadline pass without submitting a renewal application or completing CE. What is the status of her license?
questions.correct_answer: The license is placed on inactive status
distractors: The license is permanently revoked by IDFPR
The license remains active for a 90-day grace period
The license converts to a leasing agent license
questions.explanation: When an Illinois broker fails to renew by the deadline, the license is placed on inactive status. The broker cannot practice real estate until CE requirements are met and the renewal application is submitted. The license is not permanently revoked, but the broker must satisfy all outstanding requirements to reactivate.

questions.question_text: A broker changes sponsoring managing brokers. Who must notify IDFPR of the change?
questions.correct_answer: Both old and new managing brokers
distractors: Only the broker who is changing sponsors
Only the new sponsoring managing broker
questions.explanation: When an Illinois broker changes sponsoring managing brokers, both the departing and receiving managing brokers must notify IDFPR. This dual notification ensures accurate records of which managing broker currently supervises each licensee.

questions.question_text: An Illinois broker moves to a new address but waits 60 days to notify IDFPR. Has the broker complied with state requirements?
questions.correct_answer: No, changes must be reported within 30 days
distractors: Yes, 60 days is within the allowed timeframe
Yes, address changes only apply at renewal
No, changes must be reported within 10 days
questions.explanation: Illinois requires licensees to report name or address changes to IDFPR within 30 days. Waiting 60 days exceeds this deadline and constitutes a violation of the reporting requirement.

questions.question_text: What document must an Illinois broker obtain from a managing broker in order to lawfully practice real estate?
questions.explanation: Illinois requires every broker to hold a sponsor card from a sponsoring managing broker before engaging in licensed real estate activities. Without an active sponsor card on file with IDFPR, the broker cannot legally practice.
